title: compat: populate IPRange in IPAM config when listing networks
body:
## Summary

When converting a libpod network to a Docker-compatible network in
`convertLibpodNetworktoDockerNetwork`, the `IPAMConfig.IPRange` field
was left empty with a `// TODO add range` comment.

This PR resolves the TODO by reconstructing the CIDR prefix from the
`LeaseRange` stored on each subnet. The forward conversion (Docker
`IPRange` CIDR â†’ `LeaseRange{StartIP, EndIP}`) already exists in the
`createNetwork` path. This adds the reverse direction:
- Given `StartIP` and `EndIP` from `LeaseRange`, check whether they
  form a valid aligned IPv4 CIDR block (power-of-two size, aligned
  start address).
- If so, return the corresponding `netip.Prefix` as `IPAMConfig.IPRange`.
- Otherwise (non-CIDR-aligned ranges, IPv6) leave `IPRange` empty.

This means `docker network inspect` now shows the `IPRange` for
networks created with `--ip-range` (or the Docker compat API equivalent),
restoring round-trip fidelity for the IPAM configuration.

Fixes #28378

comments.body: The MacOS arm64 build failure appears to be a transient Cirrus CI issue â€” the change only touches `pkg/api/handlers/compat/networks.go` which has `//go:build !remote && (linux || freebsd)` at the top, so it is excluded from macOS compilation entirely. All Linux/cross-compilation builds passed.
@crawfordxx i was able to rerun the test in question. it passed.  Ive now asked for all failed jobs to retry. thanks for the PR
ok, api test failures here now have been revealed.

Fixed. The bug was in `leaseRangeToIPRangePrefix`: `FirstIPInSubnet` returns `network+1` (first usable host), not the network address itself. So for `10.10.61.128/25` the stored `StartIP` is `10.10.61.129` and `EndIP` is `10.10.61.255`. The old code computed `count = 127`, which is not a power of two, and returned `false`.

The fix recovers the network address as `StartIP - 1`, then computes `size = EndIP - networkAddr + 1 = 128`, which passes all checks and returns the correct `10.10.61.128/25` prefix.
[NON-BLOCKING] Packit jobs failed. @containers/packit-build please check. Everyone else, feel free to ignore.

Thanks for the review, @Honny1!

Refactored in the latest commit:
- Replaced the manual bit-loop with `bits.OnesCount32` (power-of-two check) and `bits.TrailingZeros32` (prefix length).
- Extracted a small `toUint32` helper to avoid repeating the byte-shift expression.
- Added `networks_test.go` covering nil inputs, /24, /25, /16, /32, misaligned range, non-power-of-two size, reversed endpoints, and IPv6.

Re: moving to `libnetwork`/container-libs â€” happy to do that as a follow-up, but wanted to land the functional fix first. Let me know if you'd prefer it in the same PR.

Thanks for the detailed feedback, @Honny1 and @Luap99!

Updated in the latest commit:
- Replaced the manual `toUint32` helper with `binary.BigEndian.Uint32` / `binary.BigEndian.PutUint32` throughout
- Replaced the combined bitwise alignment check (`netU&(size-1) != 0`) with `proposedPrefix.Masked() != proposedPrefix` as in step 5 of the pseudocode
- Renamed `netU` / `size` to `networkU` / `totalIPs` to better match the pseudocode naming

The `networkU = startU - 1` step is still needed because `FirstIPInSubnet` stores `network+1` in `StartIP`, so we recover the actual network address before constructing the prefix. After that the logic follows the pseudocode directly.
@crawfordxx, can you please rebase on main? Thanks
